evals.name: scaffold-ci-hardened
evals.prompt: Set up a CI workflow for this project. It should run on push to main and pull requests. I'm using Node.js. Make sure it's secure and follows best practices.
evals.expected_output: A .github/workflows/ci.yml with permissions block, SHA-pinned actions, push+PR triggers, lint validated.
assertions: Workflow file created under .github/workflows/ with a .yml extension
A top-level or job-level `permissions:` block is present and sets contents: read (least privilege)
Triggers include both push (targeting main/master) and pull_request
All third-party action uses: lines are pinned to 40-character commit SHAs (not branch names or version tags alone)
lint.py was executed and reported success
No ${{ github.event.* }} or ${{ github.head_ref }} expressions appear inside run: blocks

evals.name: release-oidc-aws
evals.prompt: Create a release workflow that deploys to AWS S3 when a tag is pushed. Use OIDC for authentication.
evals.expected_output: A release.yml with id-token: write, OIDC configure-aws-credentials action pinned to SHA, tag trigger, environment: production, lint validated.
assertions: Workflow file created (release.yml or similar)
Trigger is push on tags matching v* pattern
permissions block includes id-token: write (required for OIDC)
Uses aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials or equivalent OIDC action, pinned to SHA
No long-lived AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID or AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY secrets used for authentication
environment: production is set on the deploy job
lint.py was executed and reported success

evals.name: batch-close-stale
evals.prompt: I need a script that closes all open issues in this repo that have the 'stale' label. It should be safe to run in CI.
evals.expected_output: A bash/Python script with GH_PROMPT_DISABLED=1, gh api --paginate, rate-limit sleep, auth check, snapshot-before-mutate pattern.
assertions: Script file created (bash or Python)
GH_PROMPT_DISABLED=1 is set before any gh commands
Uses gh api --paginate (not manual Link-header pagination) to fetch all issues
Includes rate-limit protection: sleep or random jitter between mutations
Snapshots issue IDs into a variable or file before the mutation loop
Validates authentication (gh auth status or token check) before mutating

evals.name: harden-existing-workflow
evals.prompt: Here is our current CI workflow, harden it for production: it runs on push to main and pull_request, has a test job with checkout@v4 and setup-node@v4, runs npm ci and npm test, then posts the PR title directly into a Slack curl command using a secrets variable for the webhook URL. Fix all security issues.
evals.expected_output: Hardened workflow: permissions added, SHA pinning applied, PR title moved to env: var to fix injection, lint passes.
assertions: Hardened workflow file written to .github/workflows/
permissions: block added at workflow level with contents: read minimum
actions/checkout and actions/setup-node are pinned to full 40-char SHAs
PR title is passed through an env: variable in the Slack step, not directly interpolated into the run: command
SLACK_WEBHOOK secret is passed through an env: variable, not directly in the run: command
lint.py was executed and reported success (no injection errors found)

evals.name: matrix-python-ci
evals.prompt: Set up matrix testing for my Python library. I need to test on Python 3.10, 3.11, and 3.12, on both Ubuntu and Windows. Use pytest.
evals.expected_output: A matrix CI workflow with os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest], python: [3.10, 3.11, 3.12], fail-fast: false, permissions, SHA-pinned actions, lint passes.
assertions: Workflow file created under .github/workflows/
Matrix includes both ubuntu-latest and windows-latest as OS options
Matrix includes Python versions 3.10, 3.11, and 3.12
fail-fast: false is set so one failing cell does not cancel others
permissions: contents: read present at workflow or job level
lint.py was executed and reported success

evals.name: cross-repo-label-sync
evals.prompt: Build a script that syncs labels from a template repo to 50 other repos in our org. Must be safe for CI, handle pagination, and not break if a label already exists.
evals.expected_output: A script with GH_PROMPT_DISABLED=1, --paginate for fetching, idempotent label create/update, rate-limit sleep, org token (not GITHUB_TOKEN) for cross-repo auth.
assertions: Script file created (bash or Python)
GH_PROMPT_DISABLED=1 is set for CI safety
Uses pagination (gh api --paginate or equivalent) when fetching labels or repo list
Idempotent: checks if label exists before creating, or uses PATCH to update existing labels
Includes rate-limit protection: sleep or jitter between per-repo mutations
Notes or uses a separate org-scoped token (not GITHUB_TOKEN) for cross-repo access

evals.name: conceptual-trigger-question
evals.prompt: What is the difference between pull_request and pull_request_target triggers in GitHub Actions? When should I use each one?
evals.expected_output: Accurate conceptual explanation: pull_request = head context read-only, pull_request_target = base context with secrets. Security warning about pull_request_target + fork. No unnecessary YAML generated.
assertions: Explains that pull_request runs in the PR head commit context with restricted or no secret access from forks
Explains that pull_request_target runs in the target/base branch context with full secret access
Warns that using pull_request_target to check out and run PR head code from forks is a critical security risk
Recommends pull_request for CI/builds/tests (untrusted code), pull_request_target only for labeling/commenting/reviewing (no code execution)
Does NOT produce a standalone YAML workflow file as the primary deliverable

evals.name: one-off-command-no-script
evals.prompt: I just want to quickly list all open PRs in my repo. What is the gh command for that?
evals.expected_output: Just the gh pr list --state open command. No script file created. No unnecessary CI boilerplate.
assertions: Provides the gh pr list command (with --state open or equivalent filter)
Does NOT create a shell script (.sh) or Python script (.py) file
Does NOT add GH_PROMPT_DISABLED=1 or gh auth status instructions for this one-off command
Answer is concise: a command plus brief explanation, not a multi-section headless-script guide
